concept: Omnigent is a Python‑based meta‑harness that unifies multiple LLM agent SDKs (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Pi, etc.) behind a common executor and session layer.
inspiration_angle: Omnigent blends the LangChain‑style tool‑calling model with a real‑time collaborative session layer, taking the plug‑in pattern seen in VS Code extensions and applying it to LLM SDKs, thereby enabling on‑the‑fly swapping of vendor back‑ends without code changes.

concept: A portable AI‑agent skill that enforces a minimalist "lazy senior dev" mode, automatically reducing generated code to the smallest viable implementation.
inspiration_angle: A cross‑agent "lazy senior developer" enforcement layer that codifies YAGNI and standard‑library‑first principles as a reusable skill set, turning the LLM from a code generator into a code optimizer that automatically emits the most concise, dependency‑free solution.

concept: A compile‑time TeX‑to‑MathML converter that turns LaTeX formulas into native MathML markup for Markdown and web pages.
inspiration_angle: A dedicated TeX‑to‑MathML compiler that treats MathML as the final layout target rather than an intermediate format, positioning the library as a build‑time optimizer for native‑MathML browsers – a distinct approach from KaTeX/MathJax which remain runtime layout engines.
